skill.name: coda-automation
skill.description: Automate Coda document, table, row, formula, sharing, export, and publishing workflows through Rube MCP and Composio.
skill.summary: Automate Coda workspace operations through Rube MCP, including document search, table updates, exports, permissions, and publishing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many Ruby backtick executions, but the evidence is markdown inline code and fenced workflow text, not executable shell code. The confirmed risk is networked Coda automation through Rube MCP, including write, sharing, export, and public publishing workflows that can affect sensitive workspace data.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Access to Coda Data
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and directs users to add the rube.app MCP endpoint. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it sends Coda workflow requests through a third-party connector and depends on user-authorized Coda access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly requires Rube MCP and provides a network endpoint. The risk is connector-mediated access to user-authorized Coda data, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Coda Write, Sharing, and Publishing Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill includes Coda row upserts, permission grants, exports, and public publishing workflows. These are expected capabilities, but mistakes can expose or modify workspace data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences directly include data mutation, permission changes, export, and publishing. These capabilities are legitimate but carry clear data exposure risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static backtick findings are markdown inline-code references to Coda and Rube tool names or fenced workflow steps. I did not find evidence of Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited backticks are markdown formatting around tool names and numbered workflow examples. SKILL.md contains instructions, not executable Ruby code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography finding points to the skill description. I did not find cryptographic code, hashing logic, encryption settings, or weak algorithm usage at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a plain natural-language description of Coda automation. It contains no crypto API, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The static reconnaissance findings are instructions to find a table or column by name inside Coda metadata. This is normal document automation, not host system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines refer to locating Coda table and column identifiers after listing Coda metadata. They do not inspect the local machine, network, users, processes, or environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked MCP Connector Required
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on a remote MCP endpoint for Coda automation. Users should understand that Coda operations are mediated by the configured connector.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly documented. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a confirmed network dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace Sharing and Publishing Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can guide adding permissions and publishing Coda documents. These actions can expose content if used on the wrong document.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflows include permission grants and public publishing. The risk depends on user intent and target document sensitivity.

content.value_statement: Coda work often requires careful document, table, and sharing operations across many screens. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Coda workflows using Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Searches and lists Coda documents available to the connected account.
Resolves Coda browser links into document, page, and table identifiers.
Lists pages, tables, columns, rows, formulas, and sharing metadata.
Guides row insert and update workflows with key column matching.
Starts and monitors Coda content exports to HTML or Markdown.
Supports permission, publishing, unpublishing, and custom domain workflows.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP server and an authenticated Coda connection.
Tool schemas may change, so the skill instructs agents to search tools first.
It cannot bypass Coda account permissions or workspace policy controls.
Publishing and sharing actions still require careful user confirmation.

use_cases.title: Maintain Team Tables
use_cases.description: Update project, inventory, or task rows in Coda after validating table and column identifiers.

use_cases.title: Export Knowledge Pages
use_cases.description: Export selected Coda documents or pages to Markdown or HTML for review, backup, or publishing workflows.

use_cases.title: Review Workspace Access
use_cases.description: Inspect Coda sharing settings and prepare permission changes before granting access or publishing a document.

prompt_templates.title: Find Coda Documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Coda automation skill to search my accessible Coda docs for [topic]. List matching docs and suggest the best next action.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a Table
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Coda automation skill to resolve this Coda link: [url]. Then list tables, columns, and sample rows for the target table.

prompt_templates.title: Update Rows Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Coda automation skill to upsert rows into [table]. Verify key columns first, preview intended changes, then ask before writing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Publishing and Sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Coda automation skill to review sharing and publishing settings for [doc]. Identify public exposure and propose safer settings.

output_examples.input: Search for Coda docs about quarterly planning.
output_examples.output: A short list of matching documents, each with its visible title, available owner details, and recommended follow-up action.

output_examples.input: Prepare to update status rows in a project tracker.
output_examples.output: A validation summary that identifies the document, table, key columns, target rows, and changes that need approval before writing.

output_examples.input: Review whether this Coda document is safe to publish.
output_examples.output: A publishing readiness note that covers sharing settings, public access risks, category choices, and confirmation points.

best_practices: Search available Rube tools first so requests use current Coda schemas.
Resolve browser links before table, row, column, export, or publishing actions.
Preview row updates, permission grants, and publishing changes before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not publish or share a Coda document without confirming the exact document and audience.
Do not rely on display names when stable document, table, column, or row IDs are available.
Do not run bulk upserts before checking column types and unique key columns.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Coda API key?
faq.answer: It uses Rube MCP with a connected Coda account. The skill says no separate API key is needed for the MCP setup.

faq.question: Can it edit Coda table rows?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents row upsert workflows that can insert or update rows after columns and key columns are identified.

faq.question: Can it export Coda content?
faq.answer: Yes. It can start an export job and poll status for HTML or Markdown output.

faq.question: Can it manage sharing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect sharing metadata and guide permission grants through Coda tools.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it uses a network connector and can change, export, share, or publish workspace data.

faq.question: What should users confirm before using it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the target document, table, row keys, recipients, and publishing status before any write or sharing action.
